perturbation.original_text: 2.3 "PRODUCT(S)" means McDATA hardware, Software, and related features, conversions, and options, as listed in the Addendum and further provided through Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 "PRODUCT(S)" means McDATA hardware and related features, conversions, and options, as listed in the Addendum and further provided through Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Software' from the definition of "PRODUCT(S)", we create an in-text contradiction because Section 7 of the main agreement, 'END USER CUSTOMER SOFTWARE LICENSE', becomes ambiguous. It references 'Product(s) that embody or include Software' without Software being explicitly included in Products, leading to uncertainty in whether the license terms apply. This creates an enforcement issue as to whether the reseller is authorized to sell software or not and if the end-user customer is using it legally, based on the end user license agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. END USER CUSTOMER SOFTWARE LICENSE Product(s) that embody or include Software will be delivered with a shrink-wrap or click-through software license which shall govern End User Customer's use of the Software. McDATA or its suppliers retain title in and to the Software and all intellectual property rights, and no title to the Software or intellectual property rights is transferred to the End User Customers. Such Software is proprietary to, trade secret of, and copyrighted by McDATA or its suppliers.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'Software' from the definition of 'PRODUCT(S)' in Section 2.3 directly contradicts Section 7, 'END USER CUSTOMER SOFTWARE LICENSE'. Section 7 explicitly refers to 'Product(s) that embody or include Software,' but the revised Section 2.3 no longer includes Software as part of the definition of 'PRODUCT(S)'. This omission creates a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding whether the software license applies, creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: INITIAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: Reseller must successfully complete both Sales and Technical Foundation training, for each McDATA authorized sales location, as follows, within ninety (90) days of the effective date of this Agreement. Sales: All Sales Reps/sales location McDATA Solution (web-based) Technical: 3 individuals Certified Professional Accelerated Track (CPAT) McDATA will waive the charges associated with the above listed required technical courses for three (3) individuals at Reseller's organization. McDATA may, at its sole discretion, provide additional education incentives to Reseller based upon sales performance. These incentives, if made available, will be communicated via Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.changed_text: INITIAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: Reseller may complete sales and technical training, for each McDATA authorized sales location, as follows, within ninety (90) days of the effective date of this Agreement. Sales: All Sales Reps/sales location McDATA Solution (web-based) Technical: 3 individuals Certified Professional Accelerated Track (CPAT) McDATA may waive the charges associated with the above listed required technical courses for three (3) individuals at Reseller's organization. McDATA may, at its sole discretion, provide additional education incentives to Reseller based upon sales performance. These incentives, if made available, will be communicated via Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Reseller must successfully complete' to 'Reseller may complete' transforms the education requirements from mandatory to optional. This directly contradicts Section 5.3 of the main agreement, 'EDUCATIONAL REQUIREMENTS. Reseller agrees to participate in McDATA-speciflad educational courses for purposes of training Reseller's personnel', which states that the reseller agrees to participate. Since the premier addendum is part of the Reseller agreement, there's a contradiction. This makes compliance and enforcement uncertain, as the main agreement requires participation, but the addendum suggests it's optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 EDUCATIONAL REQUIREMENTS. Reseller agrees to participate in McDATA-speciflad educational courses for purposes of training Reseller's personnel. Educational course requirements will be provided in a Channel Notification Document and must be satisfied by Reseller to maintain its status as a McDATA authorized Reseller McDATA will provide to Reseller without tuition fee, at a McDATA designated training site, the required educational courses identified on the Addendum.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the reseller 'must successfully complete' the training, making it mandatory. The changed text alters this to 'Reseller may complete,' rendering the training optional. This directly contradicts Section 5.3, which states, 'Reseller agrees to participate in McDATA-speciflad educational courses.' The change introduces a strong contradiction because the main agreement requires participation, while the addendum, through the modification, makes it optional, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: McDATA reserves the right to change the Premier Partner criteria each January or upon sixty (60) days advanced notice through a Channel Notification Document. If Reseller fails to comply with any of the above requirements, McDATA reserves the right to change the Reseller Designation and Reseller will become eligible for only those programs applicable to the new Reseller Designation.
perturbation.changed_text: McDATA reserves the right to change the Premier Partner criteria each January or upon sixty (60) days advanced notice through a Channel Notification Document. If Reseller fails to comply with any of the above requirements, McDATA reserves the right to re-evaluate the Reseller Designation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'McDATA reserves the right to change the Reseller Designation and Reseller will become eligible for only those programs applicable to the new Reseller Designation' to 'McDATA reserves the right to re-evaluate the Reseller Designation', the consequence of non-compliance is no longer clearly defined. The original text specifies a change in designation and eligibility for programs. The changed text only mentions re-evaluation, leaving the actual consequences ambiguous. Since section 4.4 of the main agreement states: 'EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Termination of this Agreement shall not limit either party from pursuing any other remedies available to it, Inducing injunctive relief. The termination of this Agreement simultaneously terminates all programs and incentives McDATA offered to Reseller through Channel Notification Documents and voids any unused program account balances.', it creates a conflict because it's unclear if the Reseller will keep the termination programs if it only re-evaluates the Reseller designation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Termination of this Agreement shall not limit either party from pursuing any other remedies available to it, Inducing injunctive relief. The termination of this Agreement simultaneously terminates all programs and incentives McDATA offered to Reseller through Channel Notification Documents and voids any unused program account balances. Upon cancellation, termination or expiration of this McDATA Corporation Standard Terms OF Reseller Agreement JUNE 2004 2 Agreement, Reseller agrees to immediately pay all monies due to McDATA under this Agreement. The parties' rights and obligations under Sections 8,9 and 10 hereof shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the consequences of non-compliance, making them ambiguous by changing 'McDATA reserves the right to change the Reseller Designation and Reseller will become eligible for only those programs applicable to the new Reseller Designation' to 'McDATA reserves the right to re-evaluate the Reseller Designation'. This contradicts section 4.4, which states the termination of the agreement terminates all programs and incentives, as it's now unclear whether the Reseller retains the benefits when its designation is merely re-evaluated, causing a conflict. The removal of the original text's specificity creates uncertainty and potential for dispute about which terms should be enforced.
